user_task: Every retrieved result includes citation metadata (source + page/offset).
source: JTBD & user tasks tab, RETRIEVE bucket; seed queries from 'NeMo Retriever — JTBD seed queries' tab, RETRIEVE → citation/provenance ('I want exact page references and quoted excerpts for every claim').

success_criteria.quality: OPERATIONAL pass ONLY (no accuracy / RAGAS gate per spec). Binary pass/fail on the citation invariant: EVERY hit returned by `retriever query` carries a non-null `source` and a valid 1-indexed integer `page_number`, and each `source` resolves to a real ingested file. A single null citation field on ANY returned hit fails the whole run. Answer correctness is NOT graded here.
success_criteria.time: RETRIEVE non-agentic — wall clock per query <= 1 min
success_criteria.trigger_rate: >= 95% — a 'give me page references / quoted excerpts for every claim' prompt must fire the skill
success_criteria.subcommand_accuracy: >= 90% — must run `retriever ingest` then `retriever query` (with `--top-k`); query table-name must match the ingest table-name

environment.install: any supported install (hosted CPU or local GPU) — this suite is host-agnostic; the citation invariant is asserted at the CLI output layer and is independent of where embedding/extraction run.
environment.cli_hit_shape: Each `retriever query` hit is exactly {source, page_number, text}. `source` is the ingested file path/name; `page_number` is an int, 1-indexed. There is NO `metadata` / `_distance` key at the CLI layer.
environment.citation_invariant: OPERATIONAL invariant under test: for every hit, source != null AND page_number is an int >= 1 AND source resolves to a file that was actually ingested into the queried table. Checked on 100% of returned hits — not a sample.
environment.pdf_page_note: The spec models a citation as `<source_id, page_number, pdf_page, filename>` where pdf_page is e.g. WALMART_2017_10K_42. The shipped CLI hit does NOT expose `pdf_page` (or `source_id` / `filename`) directly — it exposes only {source, page_number, text}. pdf_page is DERIVABLE as `<basename(source)>_<page_number>` (e.g. source=woods_frost.pdf, page_number=2 -> woods_frost_2); source_id/filename collapse onto `source`. This suite asserts the two primitives the CLI actually emits (source + page_number) and derives/validates pdf_page from them; it does not assert a literal `pdf_page` field because the CLI does not return one.

cases.title: Single query, single hit carries source + page_number
cases.complexity_axis: Satisfies the citation-metadata user task in its simplest form: one query, one hit. ADDS over nothing (baseline rung) — establishes that a single returned hit carries a non-null `source` and a valid 1-indexed `page_number` (and that pdf_page is derivable as woods_frost_<page_number>). Operational-only: the excerpt's correctness is not graded.
cases.prompt: What does woods_frost.pdf say about the woods? I want an exact page reference and a quoted excerpt for the claim.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "What does woods_frost.pdf say about the woods?" --top-k 1

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever.
expected_output.query_shape: JSON array with exactly 1 hit; the hit is {source, page_number, text}
expected_output.citation_check: hit.source == woods_frost.pdf (non-null); hit.page_number is an int in {1,2}; derived pdf_page = woods_frost_<page_number>
expected_output.answer_note: (not graded) excerpt is expected to be Frost text, e.g. p1 poem text

pass_when: ingest + query round-trip completes and returns >= 1 hit
the returned hit has a non-null `source` equal to woods_frost.pdf
the returned hit has an integer `page_number` >= 1
derived pdf_page (woods_frost_<page_number>) is well-formed
query wall clock <= 1 min

cases.title: top-k=10 over a multi-page doc — ALL 10 hits carry non-null source + page_number
cases.complexity_axis: Satisfies the user task at scale on ONE document. ADDS over rung 1: the 100%-coverage dimension — not one hit but ALL of top-k=10 hits (spanning multiple pages of a 3-page doc) must each carry a non-null source and a valid 1-indexed page_number. A single null on any of the 10 fails the run.
cases.prompt: Quote me what multimodal_test.pdf says across the whole document — give exact wording and a page number for every excerpt. Return up to 10 passages.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "What does this document say? quote every section with its page" --top-k 10

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever.
expected_output.query_shape: JSON array of up to 10 hits; each {source, page_number, text}
expected_output.citation_check: for EVERY hit: source == multimodal_test.pdf (non-null) AND page_number is an int in {1,2,3}; page_number values are expected to vary across hits (doc is 3 pages)
expected_output.coverage_rule: 100% of returned hits must satisfy the check; any single hit with null source or null/zero/non-int page_number = FAIL

pass_when: query returns multiple hits (up to 10) over the multi-page doc
EVERY returned hit has non-null `source` == multimodal_test.pdf
EVERY returned hit has an integer `page_number` >= 1
no returned hit is missing either citation field (single miss = run FAIL)
query wall clock <= 1 min

cases.title: Multi-doc corpus — every hit's source resolves to a real ingested file
cases.complexity_axis: Satisfies the user task across a MULTI-DOCUMENT corpus. ADDS over rung 2: the resolvability dimension — with 3 different source files in one index, every hit's `source` must not just be non-null but must resolve to one of the actually-ingested files (woods_frost.pdf / multimodal_test.pdf / table_test.pdf), i.e. no fabricated/dangling citations.
cases.prompt: Across all three of my PDFs, pull supporting passages with exact page references and quoted excerpts for every claim. Return up to 10 results.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "supporting passages with page references across all documents" --top-k 10

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 3 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (branch_summary pdf:3)
expected_output.query_shape: JSON array of up to 10 hits; each {source, page_number, text}
expected_output.citation_check: for EVERY hit: source resolves to exactly one of {woods_frost.pdf, multimodal_test.pdf, table_test.pdf} AND page_number is a valid 1-indexed int within that file's page range (woods_frost <=2, multimodal_test <=3, table_test ==1)
expected_output.resolvability_rule: each cited source must correspond to a real row in the queried table; a source not among the ingested files = FAIL

pass_when: single `retriever ingest data/` ingests all 3 PDFs into one table
EVERY returned hit's `source` resolves to one of the 3 ingested files
EVERY returned hit has an integer `page_number` >= 1 within that file's page range
no hit cites a source that was not ingested (dangling citation = run FAIL)
query wall clock <= 1 min

cases.title: page_number correctness across pages — a page-3 fact cites page 3
cases.complexity_axis: Satisfies the user task's correctness-of-the-page-offset dimension. ADDS over rung 3: not just that page_number is present/valid, but that it is the RIGHT page — a fact that lives only on page 3 of the 3-page doc (the 'Conclusion' / 'Chart 2') must come back with page_number == 3, proving the citation points to the actual source page, not a constant.
cases.prompt: On what page of multimodal_test.pdf is the conclusion, and what does it say? Give me the exact page number and a quoted excerpt.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "What is the conclusion of the document?" --top-k 5

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever.
expected_output.query_shape: JSON array of up to 5 hits; each {source, page_number, text}
expected_output.page_correctness: the hit whose text contains the page-3-only 'Conclusion' / 'Chart 2 ... average frequency ranges for speaker drivers' content carries page_number == 3 (derived pdf_page = multimodal_test_3)
expected_output.citation_check: all returned hits still carry non-null source + valid 1-indexed page_number

pass_when: the conclusion passage (page-3-only content) comes back with page_number == 3
derived pdf_page for that hit == multimodal_test_3
every returned hit still carries non-null `source` + integer `page_number` >= 1
query wall clock <= 1 min
cases.known_caveat: Page mapping depends on extraction chunking; if a chunk straddles a page boundary the engine assigns the chunk's primary page. The page-3 'Conclusion' text is unique to p3, so the cited page_number for that passage must be 3 — a 1 or 2 would indicate a page-offset bug.

cases.title: Acceptance: strict run — every result across docs/pages/content-types carries resolvable citation metadata; any null = FAIL
cases.complexity_axis: Composed operational-pass / acceptance gate for the row. ADDS over rung 4: it combines multi-doc + multi-page + multiple content types (text + table + chart from the 3 PDFs) into ONE strict top-k=10 run and enforces the full invariant simultaneously — every returned hit, regardless of which doc / page / content type it came from, must carry a non-null source AND a valid 1-indexed page_number AND resolve to a real ingested row. A single null on a single hit fails the entire run. Operational-only: no answer-accuracy gate.
cases.prompt: Build me a fully cited evidence set from all my documents: for every passage you return, I need the source file and the exact page number, with no exceptions. Return up to 10 passages and span text, tables, and charts.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "fully cited evidence across text tables and charts" --table-name cite_audit --top-k 10 --content-types text,table,chart

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 3 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/cite_audit. (branch_summary pdf:3)
expected_output.query_shape: JSON array of up to 10 hits; each {source, page_number, text}
assertions: query reads the SAME table (cite_audit) the ingest wrote — not the default nemo-retriever
EVERY returned hit has a non-null `source` that resolves to one of the 3 ingested files
EVERY returned hit has an integer `page_number` >= 1 within that file's page range
hits span multiple source files and multiple pages (and, with --content-types text,table,chart, multiple content types)
derived pdf_page (<basename>_<page_number>) is well-formed for every hit
ANY single hit missing source or page_number = FAIL for the whole run

pass_when: ingest writes lancedb/cite_audit and query reads cite_audit (table names aligned)
100% of returned hits carry non-null `source` + integer `page_number` >= 1
every hit's `source` resolves to a real ingested file; page_number within range
no returned hit is missing a citation field (single miss = run FAIL)
query wall clock <= 1 min
